---
title: "PREVENT-AD Baseline Linear Regression"
format: html
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warnings = FALSE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = here::here())
```

# Load data
```{r setting}
library(ggpubr)
library(broom)
library(car)
library(jtools)

source('workflow/scripts/config.R')

clinical_raw <- readRDS(file.path(DATA_OUTPUT_PATHS$data$cleaned, "PREVENTAD_clinical_imp_raw.rds"))
lifestyle_raw <- readRDS(file.path(DATA_OUTPUT_PATHS$data$cleaned, "PREVENTAD_lifestyle_imp_raw.rds"))
apoe <- readRDS(file.path(DATA_INTERMEDIATE_PATH, "PREVENTAD_apoe.rds"))
biomarkers <- readRDS(file.path(DATA_INTERMEDIATE_PATH, "PREVENTAD_biomarkers.rds"))
diagnosis <- readRDS(file.path(DATA_INTERMEDIATE_PATH, "PREVENTAD_diagnosis.rds"))
cogd_dat <- readRDS(file.path(DATA_OUTPUT_PATHS$data$cleaned, "PREVENTAD_cogd_scored_dat.rds"))
fhx <- readRDS(file.path(DATA_OUTPUT_PATHS$data$cleaned, "PREVENTAD_fhx_dat.rds"))
grs <- readRDS(file.path(DATA_OUTPUT_PATHS$data$cleaned, "PREVENTAD_GRS.rds"))
libra_dat <- readRDS(file.path(DATA_OUTPUT_PATHS$data$cleaned, "PREVENTAD_libra_scored_dat.rds"))
PREVENTAD_dat <- readRDS(file.path(DATA_INTERMEDIATE_PATH, "PREVENTAD_dat.rds"))


predictors_dat <- grs %>%
  left_join((clinical_raw %>%
               select(CONP_ID, Candidate_Age, Sex, Education_years) %>%
               mutate(Candidate_Age = Candidate_Age/12)),
            by="CONP_ID") %>%
  left_join(apoe, by="CONP_ID") %>%
  left_join((cogd_dat %>% 
               select(CONP_ID, score_cogd)),
            by="CONP_ID") %>%
  left_join(fhx, by="CONP_ID") %>%
  left_join((libra_dat %>% 
               select(CONP_ID, score_libra2)),
            by="CONP_ID") %>%
  select(CONP_ID, Candidate_Age, Sex, Education_years, apoe_category, 
         score_cogd, GRS, score_libra2, FDR_ext_AD)
```

# Create filtered datasets
```{r}
biomarker_cols <- c("AB_ratio_simoa_4plex", "GFAP_simoa_4plex", "NFL_simoa_4plex",
                    "ptau181_simoa_UGOT", "ptau217_simoa_UGOT", "ptau231_simoa_UGOT")

biomarkers_filtered <- biomarkers %>%
  filter(CONP_ID %in% predictors_dat$CONP_ID) %>%
  select(CONP_ID, Visit_label, biomarker_cols)

baseline_biomarkers <- biomarkers_filtered %>% 
  filter(grepl("00", Visit_label)) 

predictors_filtered_dat <- predictors_dat %>%
  filter(CONP_ID %in% baseline_biomarkers$CONP_ID)

all_dat <- predictors_filtered_dat %>%
  left_join(baseline_biomarkers, by="CONP_ID")

saveRDS(biomarkers_filtered, file.path(DATA_OUTPUT_PATHS$data$cleaned, "PREVENTAD_biomarkers_filtered.rds"))
```

# Summary tables
## Biomarker summary statistics
```{r}
# Summary statistics of biomarker data
biomarker_table1 <- biomarkers_filtered %>%
  select(CONP_ID, all_of(biomarker_cols)) %>%
  left_join((diagnosis %>%
               group_by(CONP_ID) %>%
               slice_tail()), 
            by="CONP_ID") %>%
  select(-CONP_ID, -Date, -Source_clinical_diagnosis) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by=Clinical_diagnosis,
    missing="no",
    statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd}) [{min}-{max}]",
                     all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p})"),
    label = list(AB_ratio_simoa_4plex = "Aβ42/Aβ40",
                   GFAP_simoa_4plex = "GFAP (pg/mL)",
                   NFL_simoa_4plex = "NfL (pg/mL)",
                   ptau181_simoa_UGOT = "p-tau 181 (pg/mL)",
                   ptau217_simoa_UGOT = "p-tau 217 (pg/mL)")
  ) %>%
  bold_labels() %>%
  as_gt()
biomarker_table1
# gtsave(biomarker_table1, filename = file.path(EDA_OUTPUT_PATH$tables, "PREVENT-AD_biomarker_levels.png"))
```

```{r}
obs_per_patient_with_dx <- biomarkers_filtered %>%
  left_join((diagnosis %>%
               group_by(CONP_ID) %>%
               slice_tail()), 
            by="CONP_ID") %>%
  select(CONP_ID, biomarker_cols, Clinical_diagnosis) %>%
  group_by(CONP_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      all_of(biomarker_cols),
      ~sum(!is.na(.)),
      .names = "{.col}"
    ),
    diagnosis = last(Clinical_diagnosis),  
    .groups = "drop"
  )

table1 <- obs_per_patient_with_dx %>%
  select(diagnosis, all_of(biomarker_cols)) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = diagnosis,
    type = everything() ~ "continuous",
    statistic = list(everything() ~ "{mean} ({sd})"),
    digits = list(everything() ~ 2),
    missing = "no"
  )

# Create table by diagnosis with both stats
table2 <- obs_per_patient_with_dx %>%
  select(diagnosis, all_of(biomarker_cols)) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(biomarker_cols), ~.x >= 2)) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = diagnosis,
    type = everything() ~ "dichotomous",
    statistic = list(everything() ~ "{n}"),
    value = lapply(biomarker_cols, function(x) TRUE) %>% setNames(biomarker_cols),
    missing = "no",
    label = lapply(biomarker_cols, function(x) "    N with ≥2 observations") %>%
      setNames(biomarker_cols)
  ) 

table_list <- list()
for (i in seq_along(biomarker_cols)) {
  biomarker <- biomarker_cols[i]
  
  # Table for mean (SD)
  t1 <- obs_per_patient_with_dx %>%
    select(diagnosis, all_of(biomarker)) %>%
    tbl_summary(
      by = diagnosis,
      type = everything() ~ "continuous",
      statistic = list(everything() ~ "{mean} ({sd})"),
      digits = list(everything() ~ 2),
      label = list(AB_ratio_simoa_4plex = "N of Aβ42/Aβ40 measures per participant",
                   GFAP_simoa_4plex = "N of GFAP measures per participant",
                   NFL_simoa_4plex = "N of NfL measures per participant",
                   ptau181_simoa_UGOT = "N of p-tau 181 measures per participant",
                   ptau217_simoa_UGOT = "N of p-tau 217 measures per participant"),
      missing = "no"
    )
  
  # Table for N with ≥2
  t2 <- obs_per_patient_with_dx %>%
    select(diagnosis, all_of(biomarker)) %>%
    mutate(across(all_of(biomarker), ~.x >= 2)) %>%
    tbl_summary(
      by = diagnosis,
      type = everything() ~ "dichotomous",
      statistic = list(everything() ~ "{n}"),
      value = setNames(list(TRUE), biomarker),
      missing = "no",
      label = setNames(list("N with ≥2 measures"), biomarker)
    )
  
  # Add both to list
  table_list[[2*i - 1]] <- t1
  table_list[[2*i]] <- t2
}

# Stack all tables
biomarker_table2 <- tbl_stack(table_list) %>%
  modify_column_indent(
    columns = label,
    rows = var_label %in% c("N with ≥2 measures")
  ) %>%
  modify_table_styling(
      columns = "label",
      rows = !var_label %in% c("N with ≥2 measures"),
      text_format = "bold"
      )
```

## Baseline participant characteristics
```{r}
# Patient charateristics
patient_table1 <- predictors_filtered_dat %>%
  left_join((diagnosis %>%
               select(CONP_ID, Clinical_diagnosis) %>%
               group_by(CONP_ID) %>%
               slice_tail()), 
            by="CONP_ID") %>%
  mutate(Clinical_diagnosis = if_else(is.na(Clinical_diagnosis), "CN", Clinical_diagnosis)) %>%
  select(-CONP_ID) %>%
  mutate(FDR_ext_AD = factor(FDR_ext_AD,
                             levels=c(1, 2, 3, 4),
                             labels=c("1 FDR", "1 FDR + ext", "2+ FDR", "2+ FDR + ext"))) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = Clinical_diagnosis,
    label = list(Candidate_Age = "Age",
                 Education_years = "Education (years)",
                 apoe_category = "APOE category",
                 score_cogd = "CogDrisk score",
                 score_libra2 = "LIRBA2 score",
                 FDR_ext_AD = "Family history"),
    statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd}) [{min}-{max}]",
                     all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)")
  ) %>%
  modify_footnote(all_stat_cols() ~ "Mean (SD) for continuous; n (%) for categorical") %>%
  bold_labels() %>%
  italicize_levels() %>%
  as_gt()

#table1_gtsummary <- tbl_stack(list(patient_table1, biomarker_table2)) %>% as_gt()
patient_table1
gtsave(patient_table1, filename = file.path(EDA_OUTPUT_PATH$tables, "PREVENT-AD_baseline.png"))
```



# Checking for multicollinearity
```{r}
# Check for multicollinearity among predictors
predictors_only <- all_filtered_dat %>%
  select(score_cogd, score_libra2, GRS, FDR_ext_AD) %>%
  mutate(FDR_ext_AD = as.numeric(FDR_ext_AD))

cor_df <- cor(predictors_only)  
melted_cor <- reshape2::melt(cor_df)

plot1 <- ggplot(data = melted_cor, aes(x=Var1, y=Var2, fill=value)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(value, 2)), color="white") +
  labs(title = "Correlation Heatmap of Predictors")
plot1
# ggsave(file.path(AIM1_OUTPUT_PATH$figures, "PREVENTAD_predictor_corr.png"), plot1)
```
# Checking Normality of baseline biomarker data
```{r}
plot2 <- biomarkers_longer %>%
  #select(CONP_ID, biomarker_cols) %>%
  # pivot_longer(
  #   cols = biomarker_cols,
  #   names_to = "biomarker",
  #   values_to = "measure"
  # ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = measure)) +
  geom_density() +
  facet_wrap(~ biomarker, scales="free")
plot2
# ggsave(file.path(AIM1_OUTPUT_PATH$figures, "PREVENTAD_biomarker_dist.png"), plot2)

plot3 <- baseline_biomarkers %>%
  select(CONP_ID, GFAP_simoa_4plex, NFL_simoa_4plex, ptau181_simoa_UGOT, ptau217_simoa_UGOT) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(GFAP_simoa_4plex, NFL_simoa_4plex, ptau181_simoa_UGOT, ptau217_simoa_UGOT),
    names_to = "biomarker",
    values_to = "measure"
  ) %>%
  mutate(measure_log = log(measure)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = measure_log)) +
  geom_density() +
  facet_wrap(~ biomarker, scales="free")
plot3

# log transform
baseline_biomarkers <- baseline_biomarkers %>%
  mutate(GFAP_log = log(GFAP_simoa_4plex),
         NFL_log = log(NFL_simoa_4plex),
         ptau181_log = log(ptau181_simoa_UGOT),
         ptau217_log = log(ptau217_simoa_UGOT))
```

# Baseline models
```{r}
model1 <- lm(ptau217_log ~ Candidate_Age + Sex + apoe_category, data=all_dat)
model2 <- lm(ptau217_log ~ score_cogd, data=all_dat)
model2.1 <- lm(ptau217_log ~ score_cogd + apoe_category, data=all_dat)
model3 <- lm(ptau217_log ~ score_libra2, data=all_dat)
model3.1 <- lm(ptau217_log ~ score_libra2 + Candidate_Age + Sex + apoe_category, data=all_dat)
model4 <- lm(ptau217_log ~ score_libra2 + score_cogd + apoe_category, data=all_dat)
model4.1 <- lm(ptau217_log ~ score_libra2 + score_cogd + Candidate_Age + Sex + apoe_category, data=all_dat)
model5 <- lm(ptau217_log ~ score_libra2 + score_cogd + GRS + Candidate_Age + Sex + apoe_category, data=all_dat)
model6 <- lm(ptau217_log ~ score_libra2 + score_cogd + GRS + FDR_ext_AD + Candidate_Age + Sex + apoe_category, data=all_dat)


jtools::summ(model1)
jtools::summ(model2)
jtools::summ(model2.1) 
jtools::summ(model3)
jtools::summ(model3.1)
jtools::summ(model4)
summ(model4.1)
summ(model5)
summ(model6)


# run_sequential_models <- function(biomarker_name, data) {
#   models <- list(
#     baseline = lm(as.formula(paste0(biomarker_name, " ~ Candidate_Age + Sex + apoe_category")), data = data),
#     model2 = lm(as.formula(paste0(biomarker_name, " ~ score_cogd + score_libra2")), data = data),
#     model3 = lm(as.formula(paste0(biomarker_name, " ~ score_cogd + score_libra2 + GRS")), data = data),
#     model4 = lm(as.formula(paste0(biomarker_name, " ~ score_cogd + score_libra2 + GRS + FDR_ext_AD")), data = data),
#     model5 = lm(as.formula(paste0(biomarker_name, " ~ score_cogd + score_libra2 + GRS + FDR_ext_AD + apoe_category")), data = data)
#   )
#   return(models)
# }
```

```{r}
# ============================================================================
# UNADJUSTED MODELS (Simple associations)
# ============================================================================
run_unadjusted_models <- function(biomarker_name, data) {
  predictors <- c("score_cogd", "score_libra2", "GRS", "FDR_ext_AD", "apoe_category")
  models <- list()
  
  for (pred in predictors) {
    formula <- as.formula(paste0(biomarker_name, " ~ ", pred))
    models[[pred]] <- lm(formula, data = data)
  }
  return(models)
}

# Run for all biomarkers
unadjusted_results <- lapply(biomarker_cols, run_unadjusted_models, data = all_dat)
names(unadjusted_results) <- biomarker_cols

unadjusted_tables <- lapply(names(unadjusted_results), function(biomarker) {
  tbl_merge(
    tbls = lapply(unadjusted_results[[biomarker]], tbl_regression),
    tab_spanner = c("**CogDrisk**", "**LIBRA-2**", "**GRS**", 
                    "**Family History**", "**APOE**")
  ) %>%
    modify_caption(paste0("**Unadjusted Models: ", biomarker, "**"))
})

# View individual biomarker results
unadjusted_tables[[1]]  # ptau217
unadjusted_tables[[2]]  # abeta4240
# etc.

# ============================================
# EQUENTIAL MODELS (adding predictors incrementally)
# ============================================

run_sequential_models <- function(biomarker_name, data) {
  models <- list(
    model1 = lm(as.formula(paste0(biomarker_name, " ~ score_cogd")), data = data),
    model2 = lm(as.formula(paste0(biomarker_name, " ~ score_cogd + score_libra2")), data = data),
    model3 = lm(as.formula(paste0(biomarker_name, " ~ score_cogd + score_libra2 + GRS")), data = data),
    model4 = lm(as.formula(paste0(biomarker_name, " ~ score_cogd + score_libra2 + GRS + FDR_ext_AD")), data = data),
    model5 = lm(as.formula(paste0(biomarker_name, " ~ score_cogd + score_libra2 + GRS + FDR_ext_AD + apoe_category")), data = data)
  )
  return(models)
}

# Run for all biomarkers
sequential_results <- lapply(biomarker_cols, run_sequential_models, data = all_dat)
names(sequential_results) <- biomarker_cols

# Create tables for each biomarker
sequential_tables <- lapply(names(sequential_results), function(biomarker) {
  tbl_merge(
    tbls = lapply(sequential_results[[biomarker]], tbl_regression),
    tab_spanner = c("**Model 1**", "**Model 2**", "**Model 3**", "**Model 4**", "**Model 5**")
  ) %>%
    modify_caption(paste0("**Sequential Models: ", biomarker, "**"))
})

# View individual biomarker results
sequential_tables[[1]]  # ptau217
sequential_tables[[2]]  # abeta4240
# etc.

# ============================================
# 3. MODEL FIT COMPARISON
# ============================================

compare_model_fit <- function(models, biomarker_name) {
  data.frame(
    Biomarker = biomarker_name,
    Model = paste("Model", 1:5),
    R_squared = sapply(models, function(m) summary(m)$r.squared),
    Adj_R_squared = sapply(models, function(m) summary(m)$adj.r.squared),
    AIC = sapply(models, AIC),
    N = sapply(models, function(m) length(m$residuals))
  )
}

# Create comparison table for all biomarkers
fit_comparison <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(sequential_results), function(biomarker) {
  compare_model_fit(sequential_results[[biomarker]], biomarker)
}))

print(fit_comparison)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================================
# 5. FULLY ADJUSTED MODELS (All predictors together)
# ============================================================================

# Model 1: Main predictors only
model1_ptau217 <- lm(
  log_ptau217_simoa_UGOT ~ cogdrisk_score + libra2_score + grs + 
    family_history + apoe_e4_carrier,
  data = baseline_data
)

summary(model1_ptau217)

# Model 2: Add demographic covariates
model2_ptau217 <- lm(
  log_ptau217_simoa_UGOT ~ cogdrisk_score + libra2_score + grs + 
    family_history + apoe_e4_carrier + age + sex + education,
  data = baseline_data
)

summary(model2_ptau217)

# Check multicollinearity (VIF should be < 5-10)
vif(model2_ptau217)

# ============================================================================
# 6. RUN MODELS FOR ALL BIOMARKERS (Loop approach)
# ============================================================================

# Create list to store all models
all_models <- list()

for (biomarker in paste0("log_", biomarker_cols)) {
  
  # Skip if biomarker doesn't exist
  if (!biomarker %in% names(baseline_data)) next
  
  # Model formula
  formula <- as.formula(paste0(
    biomarker, " ~ cogdrisk_score + libra2_score + grs + ",
    "family_history + apoe_e4_carrier + age + sex + education"
  ))
  
  # Fit model
  all_models[[biomarker]] <- lm(formula, data = baseline_data)
}

# ============================================================================
# 7. CREATE REGRESSION TABLES WITH GTSUMMARY
# ============================================================================

# Single biomarker table
table_ptau217 <- tbl_regression(
  model2_ptau217,
  exponentiate = FALSE,  # Set TRUE if you want exp(β) 
  label = list(
    cogdrisk_score ~ "COGDRISK Score",
    libra2_score ~ "LIBRA2 Score",
    grs ~ "Genetic Risk Score",
    family_history ~ "Family History",
    apoe_e4_carrier ~ "APOE ε4 Status",
    age ~ "Age (years)",
    sex ~ "Sex",
    education ~ "Education (years)"
  )
) %>%
  add_glance_table(
    include = c(r.squared, adj.r.squared, nobs)
  ) %>%
  bold_p(t = 0.05) %>%
  modify_caption("**Table: Predictors of log(pTau217) at Baseline**")

table_ptau217

# ============================================================================
# 8. COMPARE MULTIPLE MODELS (side by side)
# ============================================================================

# Compare unadjusted vs adjusted
tbl_merge_ptau217 <- tbl_merge(
  tbls = list(
    tbl_regression(model1_ptau217, label = list(
      cogdrisk_score ~ "COGDRISK Score",
      libra2_score ~ "LIBRA2 Score",
      grs ~ "Genetic Risk Score"
    )),
    tbl_regression(model2_ptau217, label = list(
      cogdrisk_score ~ "COGDRISK Score",
      libra2_score ~ "LIBRA2 Score",
      grs ~ "Genetic Risk Score"
    ))
  ),
  tab_spanner = c("**Unadjusted**", "**Adjusted for demographics**")
) %>%
  modify_caption("**Table: Models for log(pTau217)**")

tbl_merge_ptau217

# ============================================================================
# 9. STACK ALL BIOMARKERS IN ONE TABLE
# ============================================================================

# Create table for each biomarker and stack them
biomarker_tables <- list()

for (biomarker in names(all_models)) {
  biomarker_tables[[biomarker]] <- tbl_regression(
    all_models[[biomarker]],
    include = c(cogdrisk_score, libra2_score, grs, family_history, apoe_e4_carrier)
  )
}

# Stack all tables
stacked_table <- tbl_stack(
  biomarker_tables,
  group_header = gsub("log_", "", names(biomarker_tables))
) %>%
  modify_caption("**Table: Predictors of Baseline Biomarker Levels**")

stacked_table

# ============================================================================
# 10. MODEL DIAGNOSTICS
# ============================================================================

# Check assumptions for one model
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model2_ptau217)

# Normality of residuals
shapiro.test(residuals(model2_ptau217))

# Outliers and influential points
outlierTest(model2_ptau217)
influencePlot(model2_ptau217)

# ============================================================================
# 11. EXPORT RESULTS
# ============================================================================

# Export single table to Word
table_ptau217 %>%
  as_flex_table() %>%
  flextable::save_as_docx(path = "ptau217_regression.docx")

# Export all models to Excel
library(openxlsx)
wb <- createWorkbook()

for (biomarker in names(all_models)) {
  addWorksheet(wb, gsub("log_", "", biomarker))
  writeData(wb, gsub("log_", "", biomarker), 
            tidy(all_models[[biomarker]], conf.int = TRUE))
}

saveWorkbook(wb, "all_biomarker_models.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

# ============================================================================
# KEY CONSIDERATIONS FOR YOUR MODELS:
# ============================================================================

# 1. Log transformation: Most biomarkers are right-skewed, log-transform first
# 2. Standardize predictors: Consider z-scoring continuous predictors for 
#    easier interpretation and comparison of effect sizes
# 3. Multiple testing correction: With multiple biomarkers, consider 
#    Bonferroni or FDR correction
# 4. Check assumptions: Normality, homoscedasticity, linearity
# 5. Multicollinearity: Check VIF, especially if COGDRISK and LIBRA2 overlap
# 6. Sample size: Need ~10-15 observations per predictor
# 7. Missing data: Consider complete case vs multiple imputation

# ============================================================================
# BONUS: Standardized coefficients for comparison
# ============================================================================

# Standardize continuous predictors
baseline_data_std <- baseline_data %>%
  mutate(
    across(c(cogdrisk_score, libra2_score, grs, age, education),
           ~scale(.) %>% as.vector(),
           .names = "{.col}_std")
  )

# Run model with standardized predictors
model_std <- lm(
  log_ptau217_simoa_UGOT ~ cogdrisk_score_std + libra2_score_std + 
    grs_std + family_history + apoe_e4_carrier + age_std + sex + education_std,
  data = baseline_data_std
)

# Now coefficients are directly comparable in magnitude
tbl_regression(model_std)
```
